title: Bridge Essay: Neural substrates of verbal repetition deficits in primary progressive aphasia.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Language/Mantra** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Phase Language/Mantra bridge-v3 targeted acquisition.

**Neural substrates of verbal repetition deficits in primary progressive aphasia.** — Brain communications (2021). PMID: 33748756. DOI: 10.1093/braincomms/fcab015. Score: 28. In this cross-sectional study, we examined the relationship between cortical thickness and performance on several verbal repetition tasks in acohort of patients with primary progressive aphasia in order to test predictions generated by theoretical accounts of phonological working memory that predict phonological content buffers in left posterior inferior frontal sulcus and supramarginal gyrus.
